text.title: Security Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules grounding the security-architecture methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every service-to-service call MUST authenticate (mTLS / signed token) and be authorised; network position MUST NOT be relied on as a trust boundary.
rule.rationale: Perimeter security fails against insider threats + lateral movement; Zero-Trust is the post-2020 baseline.

rule.statement: Authentication does NOT imply authorisation. An explicit authz matrix MUST exist mapping role × resource × operation; tenant isolation MUST be enforced via row-level filters (RLS or query predicates).
rule.rationale: Most multi-tenant breaches stem from missing authz checks on authenticated requests; tenant-id filters are the primary defence.

rule.statement: Secrets MUST live in a managed provider (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, GCP Secret Manager) with rotation enabled, MUST be scoped per service, and MUST NOT live in env-vars baked into images or in repo files.
rule.rationale: Secrets in images / repos leak via build caches, registry mirrors, and forensic recovery; managed providers + rotation cap blast radius.

rule.statement: Threat model MUST walk STRIDE per asset, attacker class, and trust boundary. Each identified threat MUST map to a control or an explicit accepted-risk record.
rule.rationale: Without STRIDE per asset, common categories (Repudiation, Information Disclosure) are systematically missed.

rule.statement: Security controls MUST be testable: SAST + SCA + secret scanning + IaC scanning + DAST MUST run in CI; CI MUST fail on high-severity findings.

rationale.source: OWASP SAMM; Faion SLDC-AI security methodology.

rule.rationale: Untested controls drift; CI-tested controls catch regressions before merge.

rule.statement: Spec MUST name detection signals (alerts), incident severity tiers, paging policy, and a quarterly tabletop cadence.
rule.rationale: Reactive-only orgs lose hours during incidents; pre-defined runbooks + tabletops compress MTTD + MTTR.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (assets, role matrix, IAM provider, compliance scope), apply r1 → r6 and emit the spec.
rule.rationale: Order encodes risk: Zero-Trust first, authz second, secrets third, threat model fourth, CI fifth, response last.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) prototype + synthetic data, (b) internal corp-SSO-only, (c) recent spec still current. Exit with note.
rule.rationale: Heavy security ceremony in these contexts wastes review time.
